hero.question: Will Scottie Scheffler win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 8.3%, the market is pricing Scottie out as a viable threat in 2026, which feels like wishful thinking from bettors who want literally anyone else to win majors. He's 27, the best ball-striker alive, and majors are won by guys who show up at the right time with the right game, not by some predetermined rotation of names.

daily_take.headline: Scheffler's Masters Odds Just Jumped 730%. Here's Why You Should Fade It.
daily_take.deck: Scottie Scheffler's 2026 Masters probability spiked from 1% to 8.3% on $3.3M in volume, likely driven by recency bias after his dominant 2024-25 season. But the market is overcorrecting. Historical data shows even generational talents rarely sustain this edge across four years, and golf's parity problem means regression is baked in. Smart money should look to short Scheffler or back McIlroy, whose odds collapsed 35 points despite being statistically the better hedge.

sidebar.headline: Ceasefire odds cratered 20 points in 24 hours. Market finally pricing in what everyone knew: April 30 is an impossibly tight deadline.

sidebar.headline: McIlroy got shellacked down to 34.5% while Scheffler soared. This creates a classic mismatch: back McIlroy at a discount if you think Scheffler is overpriced.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy 2026 Masters: 34.5%
